name: Aspurgid Claimant House
note: Engine continuity label for Aspurgus as a Bosporan claimant and not a claim of settled parentage or a resolved AD 1 succession.

name: Buyeo Royal House
note: Engine continuity label for Daeso in the project's broad Buyeo tag and not a claim that Northern and Eastern Buyeo were a single uninterrupted polity.

name: Pythodorid Royal House
note: Engine continuity label for Pythodoris of Pontus and her directly attested daughter Antonia Tryphaena at Colchis; it does not claim a complete formally reconstructed dynasty.

name: Azes Dynasty
note: Engine continuity label for the coin-attested Indo-Scythian Azes sequence; its exact internal genealogy remains contested.

name: Late Indo-Greek Royal House
note: Engine continuity label for Strato II's last Indo-Greek court and not a claim of a reconstructed genealogy.

name: Persid Royal House
note: Engine continuity label for Nambed's coin-attested Persid court; it does not claim a reconstructed genealogy.

name: Characenian Royal House
note: Engine continuity label for Attambelos II's coin-attested Characenian rule; it does not claim a reconstructed genealogy.

name: Anuradhapura Royal House
note: Engine dynasty label for the AD 1 Lankan court; it does not claim a reconstructed formal dynastic name or complete genealogy.

name: Julio-Claudian
note: The start roster uses the plan's Julio-Claudian house label for Augustus and his named court.

name: Scribonian Court Line
note: Engine identity label for Scribonia in Roman-world context; it does not claim a current Roman residence a full Scribonian genealogy or a reconstructed household role.

name: Aemilian Paullus Court Line
note: Engine continuity label for the documented AD 1 ordinary consul; it does not reconstruct the Aemilian genealogy marriage or any later career.

name: Herennius Picens Court Line
note: Engine continuity label for the documented AD 1 suffect consul; it does not reconstruct the Herennii genealogy a campaign-start appointment or a residence.

name: Quinctilian Varus Court Line
note: Engine identity label for the documented Augustan official; it does not reconstruct the Quinctilii genealogy a campaign-start command or later Teutoburg outcome.

name: Valerian Messalla Court Line
note: Engine identity label for the documented Augustan general author and patron; it does not reconstruct a Valerian genealogy or a campaign-start office.

name: Livian Cultural Line
note: Engine identity label for the source-qualified Augustan historian; it does not claim a Livian genealogy a court office or a fixed AD 1 residence.

name: Ovidian Cultural Line
note: Engine identity label for the source-qualified Augustan poet; it does not claim an office a court appointment or later exile mechanics.

name: Strabonian Cultural Line
note: Engine identity label for the source-qualified Greek geographer in the Roman world; it does not claim a Strabonian genealogy a permanent Roman residence or a government role.

name: Athenodoran Cultural Line
note: Engine identity label for the source-qualified Stoic and Augustan adviser; it does not claim an AD 1 court office a permanent residence or a formal school.

name: Verrian Cultural Line
note: Engine identity label for Augustus's named tutor of Gaius and Lucius; it does not reconstruct employment timing a court office or a wider genealogy.

name: Asinian Pollio Court Line
note: Engine identity label for the source-qualified Augustan author and historian; it does not reconstruct an Asinian genealogy a current office or a literary circle.

name: Antistian Labeo Court Line
note: Engine identity label for the source-qualified Augustan jurist; it does not reconstruct an Antistian genealogy a current legal office or a school system.

name: Ateian Capito Court Line
note: Engine identity label for the source-qualified Roman jurist; it does not pre-script his AD 5 consulship later water office or a legal school.

name: Vipsanian Court Line
note: Engine family label for the three explicitly named children of Agrippa and Julia in the Augustan household; it records only that direct parentage and does not reconstruct a full Vipsanian genealogy or later adoption sequence.

name: House of Liu
note: Western Han imperial house at the Chang'an capital proxy.

name: Wang Clan
note: Consort-kin clan label for Grand Empress Dowager Wang Wang Mang his named son Wang Yu and Wang Shun; it is neither the imperial House of Liu nor a reconstruction of the clan's full genealogy.

name: Wei Maternal Line
note: Engine identity label for Emperor Ping's named mother and maternal uncles who were ordered to remain in Zhongshan; the installed Dingzhou anchor is a Lunu proxy and does not reconstruct a complete Wei genealogy or a Chang'an residence.

name: Kong Clan
note: Engine clan label for the senior Han court official Kong Guang; it records a personal identity at the Chang'an court rather than reconstructing a full Kong genealogy.

name: Zhen Court Line
note: Engine identity for Zhen Feng and his documented son Zhen Xun in the Han regency circle; it makes no claim that either was kin to Zhen Han or that it reconstructs a full Zhen genealogy.

name: Ping Court Line
note: Engine personal-identity label for Ping Yan at the Chang'an court; it does not reconstruct a Ping genealogy.

name: Liu Xin Court Line
note: Engine identity for Liu Xin and his documented son Liu Fen at the Chang'an court; it makes no claim about their relationship to the ruling House of Liu or a full Liu genealogy.

name: Wang Yi Court Line
note: Engine personal-identity label for Wang Yi at the Chang'an court; it deliberately does not assign him to the documented Wang consort-kin clan or reconstruct a genealogy.

name: Sun Jian Court Line
note: Engine personal-identity label for the Han court official Sun Jian; it neither asserts an office chronology nor conflates him with a later namesake.

name: Cui Fa Court Line
note: Engine personal-identity label for Cui Fa at the Chang'an court; it does not reconstruct a Cui genealogy.

name: Chen Chong Court Line
note: Engine personal-identity label for Chen Chong at the Chang'an court; it does not reconstruct a Chen genealogy.

name: Wang Yun Court Line
note: Engine personal-identity label for Wang Yun in the accession-credited Han court group; it neither assigns him to Wang Mang's consort kin nor reconstructs a genealogy.

name: Liu Bu'e Court Line
note: Engine personal-identity label for Liu Bu'e in the accession-credited Han court group; it neither assigns him to the imperial House of Liu nor reconstructs a genealogy.

name: Ren Cen Court Line
note: Engine personal-identity label for Ren Cen in the accession-credited Han court group; it does not reconstruct a genealogy.

name: Kong Yong Court Line
note: Engine personal-identity label for Kong Yong in the accession-credited Han court group; it does not assert kinship with Kong Guang or reconstruct a genealogy.

name: Shi Xu Court Line
note: Engine personal-identity label for Shi Xu in the accession-credited Han court group; it does not reconstruct a genealogy.

name: Wu Zhang Court Line
note: Engine personal-identity label for Wang Yu's named teacher in the early Yuan Shi court conflict; it does not reconstruct a career or genealogy.

name: Lu Kuan Court Line
note: Engine personal-identity label for Wang Yu's named relative by marriage in the early Yuan Shi court conflict; it does not reconstruct a marriage or genealogy.

name: Yang Xiong Court Line
note: Engine identity label for the documented court scholar; it does not reconstruct Yang's family office hierarchy or later Xin service.

name: Huan Tan Court Line
note: Engine identity label for the Han-Xin scholarly court figure; it does not reconstruct Huan's family a precise January office or later career.

name: Adiabenian Royal House
note: Engine continuity label for the campaign-boundary Adiabenian ruler; it does not reconstruct a genealogy marriage conversion or later succession.

name: Arsacid
note: Baghdad is the existing Ctesiphon map proxy recorded in the M3 capital registry.

name: Marcomannic Royal House
note: Engine dynasty label for Maroboduus's documented Marcomannic kingship rather than a claim of a fully reconstructed genealogy.

name: Cheruscan Nobility
note: Engine dynasty label for Arminius's aristocratic Cheruscan background rather than a claim of a formal hereditary house name.

name: Numidian Royal House
note: Juba II belongs to the Numidian royal line; Volubilis is the existing Mauretanian capital anchor.

name: Ptolemaic
note: Cleopatra Selene II's Ptolemaic house is represented at her Mauretanian court anchor.

name: Nabataean Royal House
note: Engine dynasty label for the Nabataean royal succession pending a dedicated prosopography pass.

name: Huldu Court Line
note: Engine identity label for Queen Huldu in Aretas IV's source-attested early Nabataean court; it does not reconstruct a natal dynasty or wider genealogy.

name: Kushite Royal House
note: Engine dynasty label for the Natakamani-Amanitore court pending a dedicated Kushite genealogy pass.

name: Luandi Clan
note: The Luandi clan is the Xiongnu ruling line represented by Wuzhuliu Chanyu.

name: Go Royal House
note: Yuri's Goguryeo royal house is conventionally identified with the Go line.

name: Catuvellaunian Royal House
note: Engine dynasty label for Tasciovanus and Cunobelinus pending a dedicated Iron-Age British prosopography pass.

name: Atrebatian Royal House
note: Engine royal-house label for coin-attested Tincomarus at the Atrebatic Calleva proxy; it does not reconstruct a full genealogy or succession.

name: Eppillus Court Line
note: Engine identity label for the coin-attested Eppillus in the Atrebatic start context; it asserts neither kinship with Tincomarus nor a fixed Atrebatic-Cantii territorial settlement.

name: Dubnovellaunos Court Line
note: Engine identity label for the coin-attested Dubnovellaunos in the Trinovantian start context; it asserts neither a complete genealogy nor a fixed Trinovantian-Cantii territorial settlement.

name: Addedomaros Court Line
note: Engine identity label for Addedomaros in the Trinovantian start context; the broad coin-type range and Catuvellaunian authority metadata assert neither a fixed AD 1 reign nor a genealogy territorial settlement or succession.

name: Herodian
note: The Herodian dynasty is shared by Archelaus Antipas and Philip in the AD 1 client tetrarchy.

name: Cappadocian Royal House
note: Engine dynasty label for King Archelaus of Cappadocia pending a dedicated Cappadocian genealogy pass.

name: Orontid
note: Antiochus III's Commagene royal line is conventionally identified as Orontid.

name: Sapaean
note: Rhoemetalces I's Odrysian client kingship belongs to the Sapaean dynasty.

name: Mithridatic
note: Dynamis's Bosporan royal line is represented through a conservative Mithridatic engine dynasty label.

name: Sampsigeramid
note: The Emesan royal house descends from Sampsigeramus and is represented by Iamblichus II at the AD 1 start.

name: Abgarid
note: The Abgarid house rules Osroene from Edessa at the AD 1 start.

name: Atropatenian Royal House
note: Conservative continuity label for Ariobarzanes II rather than a claim of a fully reconstructed formal dynasty name.

name: Artaxiad
note: Tigranes IV and Erato belong to the late Artaxiad royal house.

name: Pharnavazid
note: Conservative dynasty label for Pharasmanes I because the early Iberian sequence remains chronologically disputed.
